summary: The PR adds markdown support but regresses both the historical default CLI output and explicit --output json, which unchanged automation and contract surfaces still rely on.

findings.title: Changing the default output to markdown breaks the nightly no-flag consumer
findings.impact: The unchanged nightly fanout shells out to `release-readiness input.json` without `--output` and parses stdout as JSON keyed by the top-level `version` field. After this change the default path emits markdown instead, so that downstream parse fails immediately.
findings.test_gap: The CI snapshot says the downstream integration suite is skipped in this stack, so the no-flag automation contract is currently unguarded.

findings.title: The json renderer registry now routes explicit --output json through markdown
findings.impact: `export_report(..., output='json')` now returns a markdown string, and the CLI wraps that in `json.dumps`, producing a JSON string literal instead of the historical object payload. Callers that explicitly request `--output json` lose the documented machine-readable top-level fields such as `version`.
findings.test_gap: The test changes add markdown snapshot coverage only, while the documented JSON contract remains untested in this stack.
